'''Tahi Reihana''' (born 15 March 1970) is a New Zealand former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s in the [[Australian Rugby League]] premiership and [[Super League]] competitions.

==Playing career==
Beginning his First Grade career in Group 2 where he represented in 1993, playing for North Coast and winning Coffs Harbour First Grade Players Player and Player of the Year. 
In 1994 Reihana was judged to be the Most Outstanding Player at the [[South Perth Lions]] rugby league club in the [[Western Australia Rugby League]] competition.<ref>[http://www.sportingpulse.com/club_info.cgi?c=1-2127-19792-0-0&sID=17985 Most Outstanding Players] ''South Perth Lions Rugby League Club''</ref> In 1995 he made his first grade debut, playing for the new [[WA Reds|Western Reds]] franchise winning the Reserve Grade Players Player in 1995 and 1996.

In 1997 he moved to England, signing with the [[Bradford Bulls]]. He was a regular starter in the record 20 consecutive Super League wins, the current world record for top-level competition. Bradford released Reihana at the end of the 1998 season to make room in the overseas quota.<ref>[http://archive.thisisbradford.co.uk/1998/10/13/170324.html Bulls free prop Tahi] ''Telegraph & Argus'', 13 October 1998</ref>

In 2000 Reihana was playing for [[Souths Logan Magpies|Brisbane Souths]] in the [[Queensland Cup]]. He won the Pat Phelan Memorial Trophy as the club's player of the year when he was selected for the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|Aotearoa Māori]] side to contest the 2000 Rugby League World Cup.<ref name="coffey-wood-2008">{{cite book|author=John Coffey, Bernie Wood|title=100 Years: Maori Rugby League, 1908-2008|publisher=Huia Publishers|year=2008|pages=318|isbn=9781869693312|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=nklWo8vw-iIC|accessdate=30 November 2009}}</ref> While playing for Brisbane Souths, Reihana made the [[Queensland Residents rugby league team|Queensland Residents]] side in 1999 and 2000.<ref>[https://www.hotfrog.com.au/Companies/Souths-Logan-Magpies About Souths Logan Magpies] ''HotFrog''</ref> He moved to the [[Wynnum Manly Seagulls]] in 2002, and again made the Residents side.<ref>[http://www.queensland.rleague.com/clubs/wynnum.php Wynnum Manly Seagulls] ''rleague.com''</ref><ref>Javed Hamidi [http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=10686 New Zealand needs to take the next step] ''rleague.com'', 4 April 2002</ref>

==Representative career==
Reihana represented [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|Aotearoa Māori]] at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cup]], playing in three matches.
